errno(3) Library Functions Manual errno(3)

NAME

errno - Errno

SYNOPSIS

Files


file asciichat_errno.c
🚨 Custom error code system with formatted messages, thread-local storage, and errno mapping file asciichat_errno.h
⚠️‼️ Comprehensive thread-local error context system for ascii-chat

Data Structures


struct backtrace_t
struct asciichat_error_context_t
Error context structure. struct asciichat_error_stats_t
Error statistics structure.

Macros


#define SET_ERRNO(code, context_msg, ...)
Set error code with custom context message and log it, returning the error code. #define SET_ERRNO_SYS(code, context_msg, ...)
Set error code with custom message and system error context, returning the error code. #define HAS_ERRNO(var)
Check if an error occurred and get full context. #define CLEAR_ERRNO()
Clear the current error state. #define GET_ERRNO()
Get current error code (0 if no error).

Variables


__thread asciichat_error_context_t asciichat_errno_context
Thread-local error context storage. __thread asciichat_error_t asciichat_errno
Thread-local current error code.

Detailed Description

Overview

This header defines ascii-chat's centralized error handling system: a thread-local error number system with full context capture including location information, stack traces (debug builds), and system error integration. Rather than the limited errno semantics of standard C (which loses context), this system captures the full error story: where it happened, why it happened, and what the calling code was trying to do.

This is NOT a cosmetic improvement. Error handling is one of the most critical parts of ascii-chat. With 2,880+ SET_ERRNO calls throughout 250+ library files and 50+ source files, nearly every significant operation in the codebase uses this system. The densest users are network (91 calls in packet parsing alone), crypto (86+ in SSH key parsing, 86+ in crypto context, 68+ in handshakes), and utilities (53+ in IP parsing).

Why This Matters

Good error messages save hours of debugging. Without context:

  • Network packet parsing fails silently with "invalid packet"
  • Crypto handshakes break with "key exchange failed"
  • IP parsing rejects addresses with "parse error"

With this system:

  • "Invalid packet magic: 0xDEADBEEF (expected 0xCAFEBABE) [packet.c:247 in parse_packet_header]"
  • "Server signature verification FAILED - rejecting connection [client.c:892 in handle_key_exchange_reply]"
  • "Failed to parse IP address: unexpected port specifier at position 15 [ip.c:184 in parse_ipv4_address]"

Core Architecture

Thread-Local Error Context (asciichat_error_context_t):

  • Error code: One of 256 possible asciichat_error_t values
  • Location: File, line, function where error occurred (debug builds capture all three)
  • Context message: Formatted printf-style explanation of what failed and why
  • Stack trace: Full backtrace from error site (debug builds with ENABLE_ERRNO_BACKTRACES)
  • System error: Integration with errno (POSIX) and WSA errors (Windows)
  • Timestamp: When the error occurred (microseconds since epoch)

Library Interface (lib/ code):

  • Use SET_ERRNO(code, "format", ...) to set errors with context
  • Use SET_ERRNO_SYS(code, "format", ...) to capture system errno alongside app error
  • Errors bubble up through the call stack (SET_ERRNO in deeper functions sets context)
  • Higher-level code can CHECK errors and decide: propagate, recover, or fail

Application Interface (src/ code):

  • Use HAS_ERRNO(&ctx) to check if an error occurred and get full context
  • Use GET_ERRNO() to check just the error code (returns ASCIICHAT_OK if no error)
  • Use CLEAR_ERRNO() to reset error state after handling
  • Integrate with FATAL() to exit with full error details

Usage Patterns (From Real Code)

Pattern 1: Input Validation (Network Packet Parsing)

~1000+ locations - the most common pattern

if (!header || !pkt_type) {


  return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: header=%p, pkt_type=%p", header, pkt_type);
}
if (magic != PACKET_MAGIC) {


  return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid packet magic: 0x%x (expected 0x%x)",


                   magic, PACKET_MAGIC);
}
if (len > MAX_PACKET_SIZE) {


  return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Packet too large: %u > %d", len, MAX_PACKET_SIZE);
}

Pattern 2: Memory Allocation Failure (Crypto Operations)

~400+ locations

uint8_t *key = SAFE_MALLOC(key_size, uint8_t *);
if (!key) {


  return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes for ephemeral key", key_size);
}

Pattern 3: System Call Failure with errno (File I/O, Networking)

~166 locations - use SET_ERRNO_SYS to capture both errno and context

int fd = open(path, O_RDONLY);
if (fd < 0) {


  return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to open config: %s", path);


  // Logs: "Failed to open config: /etc/ascii-chat.conf (errno: 2 - No such file)"
}

Pattern 4: Error Context Propagation

~50+ locations - deepest call checks error without overwriting

// In crypto_perform_handshake() - deep function
asciichat_error_t result = validate_server_signature(sig_data, server_key);
if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {


  return result;  // Propagates original error context up the call stack
}

Pattern 5: Application-Level Error Handling

~30 locations - entry points check errors after library calls

// In src/client/main.c
asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {


  FATAL(err_ctx.code, "Connection failed: %s at %s:%d",


        err_ctx.context_message, err_ctx.file, err_ctx.line);
}

Thread Safety Guarantee

Each thread has completely independent error state via __thread storage:

  • Setting error in thread A doesn't affect thread B's error state
  • No mutex needed (no contention, no lock ordering issues)
  • Perfect for multi-threaded network servers with per-client threads

Context Capture Details

In Release Builds (NDEBUG):

  • File, line, function are NULL (stripped for performance)
  • Context message is captured (explains what went wrong)
  • System errno captured if SET_ERRNO_SYS used
  • No stack trace (compiler optimizations make backtraces unreliable)
  • Total overhead: one string allocation + context copy per error

In Debug Builds:

  • File, line, function are captured (automatic via FILE, LINE, func)
  • Context message is formatted with arguments
  • System errno and/or WSA error captured
  • Stack trace captured if ENABLE_ERRNO_BACKTRACES defined (configurable)
  • Timestamp captured for performance analysis

Error Statistics (Monitoring & Diagnostics)

The system tracks cumulative error counts per error code across all threads:

asciichat_error_stats_t stats = asciichat_error_stats_get();
printf("Total errors seen: %llu\\n", stats.total_errors);
printf("Network errors: %llu\\n", stats.error_counts[ERROR_NETWORK]);
printf("Crypto errors: %llu\\n", stats.error_counts[ERROR_CRYPTO]);

Useful for:

  • Detecting error storms (too many of one error type = bug)
  • Performance monitoring (which errors consume resources?)
  • Testing validation (no errors in unit tests = good)
  • Production alerting (sudden spike in specific error = something broke)

Common Error Codes (From error_codes.h)

  • ERROR_MEMORY: Allocation failed (malloc/calloc)
  • ERROR_INVALID_PARAM: Invalid function argument
  • ERROR_INVALID_STATE: Operation invalid in current state
  • ERROR_NETWORK_*: Network-related errors (bind, listen, connect, etc.)
  • ERROR_CRYPTO: Cryptographic operation failed
  • ERROR_CONFIG: Configuration file or parsing error
  • ERROR_AUDIO: Audio capture/playback error
  • See lib/common/error_codes.h for complete list

Debugging with Error Context

In GDB:

(gdb) print asciichat_errno_context
$1 = {code = ERROR_CRYPTO, file = "lib/crypto/handshake/client.c", line = 892,


      function = "handle_key_exchange_reply", context_message = "Signature verification failed",


      timestamp = 1730851234567890, ...}

In Running Program (if dumped by FATAL):

[ERROR] ERROR_CRYPTO: Signature verification failed
Location: lib/crypto/handshake/client.c:892 in handle_key_exchange_reply()
Timestamp: 2025-10-06 14:07:14.567890 UTC
System error: 0 (success)
Stack trace (11 frames):


  [0] handle_key_exchange_reply (lib/crypto/handshake/client.c:892)


  [1] crypto_perform_handshake (lib/crypto/crypto.c:156)


  [2] connection_authenticate (src/client/connection.c:289)


  ... (8 more frames)

Note

Use SET_ERRNO() in library code. Macros auto-capture file/line/function.

Use HAS_ERRNO() in application code to check errors after library calls.

Stack traces are only captured in debug builds with ENABLE_ERRNO_BACKTRACES.

Thread-local storage ensures thread safety automatically (no synchronization).

This is the error handling backbone of ascii-chat. Understand it well.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

void
  asciichat_clear_errno (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Clear the current error state. Clears the thread-local error
    state, resetting error code to ASCIICHAT_OK and freeing any associated
    context message. This is the low-level function used by CLEAR_ERRNO()
    macro.




Note


Error context structure is reset to default state.



Any context message is freed automatically.




Thread-safe: Uses thread-local storage.




Warning


Use CLEAR_ERRNO() macro instead of calling this
  directly.


Definition at line 233 of file
  asciichat_errno.c.


233                                  {
234   if (asciichat_errno_context.context_message) {
235     SAFE_FREE(asciichat_errno_context.context_message);
236     asciichat_errno_context.context_message = NULL;
237   }
238 
239   backtrace_t_free(&asciichat_errno_context.backtrace);
240 
241   memset(&asciichat_errno_context, 0, sizeof(asciichat_errno_context));
242   asciichat_errno_context.code = ASCIICHAT_OK;
243   asciichat_errno_context.system_errno = 0;
244   asciichat_errno_context.has_system_error = false;
245   asciichat_errno_context.has_wsa_error = false;
246   asciichat_errno_context.wsa_error = 0;
247 
248   /* Clear platform-specific error state */
249   platform_clear_error_state();
250 }






References asciichat_errno_context, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    backtrace_t_free(), platform_clear_error_state(), and
    SAFE_FREE.

Clear error code for a specific thread.

thread_id Thread ID to clear

Clears the error code for a specific thread, resetting it to

Note


Thread ID must be valid (positive integer).

void
  asciichat_errno_destroy (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Cleanup error system resources. Cleans up error system resources
    including statistics and thread error storage. Should be called at
    application shutdown after all error handling is complete.




Note


Safe to call multiple times (no-op after first call).



All error statistics and context messages are freed.




Definition at line 501 of file
  asciichat_errno.c.


501                                    {
502   backtrace_t_free(&asciichat_errno_context.backtrace);
503 
504   if (asciichat_errno_context.context_message != NULL) {
505     SAFE_FREE(asciichat_errno_context.context_message);
506   }
507 
508   // Reset the context to a clean state
509   memset(&asciichat_errno_context, 0, sizeof(asciichat_errno_context));
510   asciichat_errno_context.code = ASCIICHAT_OK;
511 
512   // Suppress any further error context allocation to prevent cleanup-phase leaks
513   // This prevents other atexit() functions from allocating new contexts after we've cleaned up
514   g_suppress_error_context = true;
515 }






References asciichat_errno_context, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    backtrace_t_free(), and SAFE_FREE.

void
  asciichat_errno_suppress (bool suppress)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Suppress error logging and reporting.


Parameters


suppress If true, suppress error logging; if
  false, enable logging


Controls whether errors are automatically logged when set. When
    suppressed, errors are still set in thread-local storage but logging is
    disabled. Useful for testing or when error logging would be too verbose.




Note


Suppression only affects automatic logging, not manual
  error checking.



Errors can still be checked using HAS_ERRNO() and
    GET_ERRNO().




Definition at line 497 of file
  asciichat_errno.c.


497                                              {
498   g_suppress_error_context = suppress;
499 }










asciichat_error_stats_t asciichat_error_stats_get (void
  )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Get current error statistics.


Returns


Current error statistics structure (copy)


Retrieves a copy of the current error statistics. Returns all
    statistics including per-error-code counts, total errors, and last error
    information.




Note


Returns a copy of statistics (caller can modify without
  affecting internal state).



Thread-safe: Can be called from any thread.

443   asciichat_error_stats_t result = g_error_stats.stats;

445 
446   return result;
447 }

void
  asciichat_error_stats_init (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Initialize error statistics system. Initializes the error
    statistics tracking system. Must be called before recording any error
    statistics. Statistics are initialized to zero.




Note


Idempotent: Safe to call multiple times (no-op after
  first call).



Thread-safe: Can be called from any thread.

  asciichat_errno.c.


365                                       {

369 }

void
  asciichat_error_stats_print (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Print error statistics to stderr. Prints comprehensive error
    statistics including per-error-code counts, total errors, and last error
    information. Useful for periodic monitoring and debugging.




Note


Statistics are formatted and printed at INFO level.



Requires statistics system to be initialized.




Definition at line 388 of file
  asciichat_errno.c.


388                                        {
389   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_error_stats.lifecycle)) {
390     log_plain("No errors recorded.\n");
391     return;
392   }
393 
394   mutex_lock(&g_error_stats.mutex);
395 
396   if (g_error_stats.stats.total_errors == 0) {
397     mutex_unlock(&g_error_stats.mutex);
398     log_plain("No errors recorded.\n");
399     return;
400   }
401 
402   // Copy stats to local variable to minimize lock hold time
403   asciichat_error_stats_t local_stats = g_error_stats.stats;
404   mutex_unlock(&g_error_stats.mutex);
405 
406   log_plain("\n=== ascii-chat Error Statistics ===\n");
407   log_plain("Total errors: %llu\n", (unsigned long long)local_stats.total_errors);
408 
409   if (local_stats.last_error_time > 0) {
410     time_t sec = (time_t)(local_stats.last_error_time / NS_PER_MS_INT);
411     struct tm tm_info;
412     if (platform_localtime(&sec, &tm_info) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
413       char time_str[64];
414       strftime(time_str, sizeof(time_str), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", &tm_info);
415       log_plain("Last error: %s (code %d)\n", time_str, (int)local_stats.last_error_code);
416     }
417   }
418 
419   log_plain("\nError breakdown:\n");
420   for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
421     if (local_stats.error_counts[i] > 0) {
422       log_plain("  %3d (%s): %llu\n", i, asciichat_error_string((asciichat_error_t)i),
423                 (unsigned long long)local_stats.error_counts[i]);
424     }
425   }
426   log_plain("\n");
427 }

    asciichat_error_stats_t::error_counts,

void
  asciichat_error_stats_record (asciichat_error_t code)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Record an error in statistics.


Parameters


code Error code to record
  (asciichat_error_t)


Records an error occurrence in the statistics system. Updates
    per-error-code count, total error count, and last error information.




Note


Thread-safe: Can be called from multiple threads
  simultaneously.



Statistics counters are updated atomically.




Definition at line 371 of file
  asciichat_errno.c.


371                                                           {
372   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_error_stats.lifecycle)) {
373     lifecycle_init(&g_error_stats.lifecycle, "error_stats");
374   }
375 
376   mutex_lock(&g_error_stats.mutex);
377 
378   if (code >= 0 && code < 256) {
379     g_error_stats.stats.error_counts[code]++;
380   }
381   g_error_stats.stats.total_errors++;
382   g_error_stats.stats.last_error_time = time_ns_to_us(time_get_realtime_ns());
383   g_error_stats.stats.last_error_code = code;
384 
385   mutex_unlock(&g_error_stats.mutex);
386 }

void
  asciichat_fatal_with_context (asciichat_error_t code, const char *
  file, int line, const char * function, const char * format, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Exit with error code and context (used by FATAL macro).


Parameters


code Error code (asciichat_error_t)


file Source file name (can be NULL)


line Line number (0 if not provided)


function Function name (can be NULL)


format Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Low-level function used by FATAL() macro. Sets error
    context and exits the program with the specified error code. In debug
    builds, prints stack trace before exiting.




Note


This function never returns (terminates program).



In debug builds, prints full error context and stack trace.




Thread-safe: Uses thread-local storage.




Warning


This function terminates the program. Use FATAL()
  macro instead of calling this directly.


Definition at line 261 of file
  asciichat_errno.c.


262                                                            {
263   (void)file;
264   (void)line;
265   (void)function;
266 
267   // Print library error context if available
268   asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
269   if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
270     log_labeled("\nasciichat_errno: libary code error context", LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "");
271     asciichat_print_error_context(&err_ctx);
272   } else {
273     log_plain("WARNING: No error context found (asciichat_errno_context.code=%d)", asciichat_errno_context.code);
274   }
275 
276   safe_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
277   log_labeled("FATAL ERROR", LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "exit code %d (%s)", (int)code, asciichat_error_string(code));
278 #ifndef NDEBUG
279   const char *relative_file = extract_project_relative_path(file);
280   log_plain("  Location: %s:%d in %s()", relative_file, line, function);
281 #endif
282 
283   if (format) {
284     va_list args;
285     va_start(args, format);
286     char *formatted_message = format_message(format, args);
287     log_plain("  Error message: %s", formatted_message);
288     SAFE_FREE(formatted_message);
289     va_end(args);
290   }
291 
292 #ifndef NDEBUG
293   // Always print platform backtrace in debug/dev builds
294   backtrace_t bt = {0};
295   backtrace_capture_and_symbolize(&bt);
296   if (bt.count > 0) {
297     backtrace_print("\nFATAL BACKTRACE", &bt, 0, 0, skip_backtrace_frame);
298     backtrace_t_free(&bt);
299   }
300 #endif
301 
302 #ifdef EMSCRIPTEN_BUILD
303   // In WASM builds, do not call exit() — it permanently kills the module runtime.
304   // Instead, set the error and return so callers can handle it gracefully.
305   SET_ERRNO(code, "FATAL (non-exit in WASM): code=%d", (int)code);
306 #else
307   exit(code);
308 #endif
309 }










asciichat_error_t asciichat_get_errno (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Get current error code.


Returns


Current error code (ASCIICHAT_OK if no error)


Returns the current thread-local error code. Returns ASCIICHAT_OK
    if no error has occurred. This is the low-level function used by
    GET_ERRNO().




Note


Thread-safe: Uses thread-local storage.



For full error context, use HAS_ERRNO() instead.

void
  asciichat_print_error_context (const asciichat_error_context_t *
  context)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Print full error context to stderr.


Parameters


context Error context to print (must not be
  NULL)


Prints comprehensive error information including error code,
    location, context message, system errors, and stack trace (if available) to
    stderr. Useful for debugging and error reporting.




Note


Stack trace is only printed in debug builds.



System errors (errno, WSA) are included if present.




Timestamp and location information are included.




Definition at line 316 of file
  asciichat_errno.c.


316                                                                              {
317   if (!context || context->code == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
318     return;
319   }
320 
321   if (context->file && context->line && context->function) {
322     log_plain("  Location: %s:%d in %s()", extract_project_relative_path(context->file), context->line,
323               context->function);
324   } else {
325     log_plain("  Location: unknown (set by system code)");
326   }
327 
328   if (context->context_message) {
329     safe_fprintf(stderr, "  %s %s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, "Context:"), context->context_message);
330     log_file("  Context: %s", context->context_message);
331   }
332 
333   if (context->has_system_error) {
334     log_plain("  System error: %s (code: %d, meaning: %s)", SAFE_STRERROR(context->system_errno), context->system_errno,
335               SAFE_STRERROR(context->system_errno));
336   }
337 
338   // Print timestamp
339   if (context->timestamp > 0) {
340     time_t sec = (time_t)(context->timestamp / NS_PER_MS_INT);
341     long usec = (long)(context->timestamp % NS_PER_MS_INT);
342     struct tm tm_info;
343     if (platform_localtime(&sec, &tm_info) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
344       char time_str[64];
345       (void)strftime(time_str, sizeof(time_str), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", &tm_info);
346       log_plain("  Timestamp: %s.%06ld", time_str, usec);
347     }
348   }
349 
350   // Print stack trace from library error
351   if (context->backtrace.count > 0) {
352     // Symbolize backtrace only when actually printing it (not at capture time)
353     backtrace_symbolize(&context->backtrace);
354     if (context->backtrace.symbols) {
355       backtrace_print("\nBacktrace from library error", &context->backtrace, 0, 0, skip_backtrace_frame);
356     }
357   }
358 }

void
  asciichat_set_errno (asciichat_error_t code, const char * file, int
  line, const char * function, const char * context_message)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Set error code with basic context.


Parameters


code Error code to set (asciichat_error_t)


file Source file name (can be NULL)


line Line number (0 if not provided)


function Function name (can be NULL)


context_message Custom context message (can be NULL, will be
  copied)


Sets the thread-local error code with basic context information.
    This is the low-level function used by SET_ERRNO() macros.




Note


In debug builds, automatically captures stack trace if
  enabled.



Context message is copied if provided (caller can free
  original).




Thread-safe: Uses thread-local storage (no synchronization
    needed).




Warning


Use SET_ERRNO() macros instead of calling this
  directly.


Definition at line 113 of file
  asciichat_errno.c.


114                                                       {
115   // Suppress error context allocation during cleanup to prevent leaks
116   if (g_suppress_error_context) {
117     return;
118   }
119 
120   // Clear any existing context message
121   if (asciichat_errno_context.context_message) {
122     SAFE_FREE(asciichat_errno_context.context_message);
123     asciichat_errno_context.context_message = NULL;
124   }
125 
126   // Set the error context
127   asciichat_errno_context.code = code;
128   asciichat_errno_context.file = file;
129   asciichat_errno_context.line = line;
130   asciichat_errno_context.function = function;
131   asciichat_errno_context.timestamp = time_ns_to_us(time_get_realtime_ns());
132   asciichat_errno_context.has_system_error = false;
133 
134   // Set the simple error code variable
135   asciichat_errno = code;
136 
137   // Copy context message if provided
138   if (context_message == NULL) {
139     log_error("context_message is NULL");
140     const char *fallback = "No context message (this is invalid - set a context message)";
141     size_t len = strlen(fallback) + 1;
142     asciichat_errno_context.context_message = SAFE_MALLOC(len, char *);
143     if (asciichat_errno_context.context_message) {
144       SAFE_STRNCPY(asciichat_errno_context.context_message, fallback, len);
145     } else {
146       log_error("SAFE_MALLOC failed for fallback context_message");
147     }
148   } else {
149     size_t len = strlen(context_message) + 1;
150     asciichat_errno_context.context_message = SAFE_MALLOC(len, char *);
151     if (asciichat_errno_context.context_message) {
152       SAFE_STRNCPY(asciichat_errno_context.context_message, context_message, len);
153     } else {
154       log_error("SAFE_MALLOC failed for context_message");
155     }
156   }
157 
158   // Capture stack trace in debug builds
159   backtrace_t_free(&asciichat_errno_context.backtrace);
160   capture_backtrace(&asciichat_errno_context.backtrace);
161 
162   // Record in statistics
163   asciichat_error_stats_record(code);
164 }

467     if (thread_errors[i].valid && thread_errors[i].thread_id == thread_id) {

469       break;
470     }

__thread asciichat_error_t asciichat_errno [extern]


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Thread-local current error code. Current error code for the
    calling thread. Set to ASCIICHAT_OK when no error. Updated automatically
    when errors are set via SET_ERRNO() macros.




Note


Use GET_ERRNO() macro to read the current error
  code.



Use CLEAR_ERRNO() macro to clear the error state.

__thread asciichat_error_context_t asciichat_errno_context
  [extern]


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/asciichat_errno.h>


Thread-local error context storage. Each thread has its own
    independent error context. This ensures thread-safe error handling without
    synchronization overhead.




Note


Use HAS_ERRNO() macro to check for errors and get
  context.



Context is automatically updated when errors are set via
    SET_ERRNO().
